question: Which drug is interacted most with proteins whose names start with 'P' or 'T' by DPI of target type? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (d:Drug)-[:DPI {display_relation: 'target'}]->(p:Protein) WHERE p.node_name STARTS WITH 'P' OR p.node_name STARTS WITH 'T' WITH d, count(DISTINCT p) AS c RETURN d.node_name ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which sweetener sweetens most food products whose allergen is Alcohol? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (s:Sweetener)-[:SWEETENS]->(f:Food_Product) MATCH (f)-[:HAS_ALLERGEN]->(a:Allergen) WHERE a.name = 'Alcohol' WITH s, COUNT(DISTINCT f) AS foodCount RETURN s.name AS Sweetener ORDER BY foodCount DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which seasoning is used most among all the food products that have no sweetener? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (f:Food_Product) WHERE NOT EXISTS {(f)<-[:SWEETENS]-(:Sweetener)} MATCH (s:Seasoning)-[:SEASONS]->(f) WITH s, COUNT(DISTINCT f) AS productCount RETURN s.name AS Seasoning ORDER BY productCount DESC LIMIT 1

question: If a person who is allergic to Dairy randomly selects a food product in this database, to what probability that this person won't be affected?
cypher: MATCH (f:Food_Product) WITH count(DISTINCT f) AS totalcount MATCH (f2:Food_Product)-[:HAS_ALLERGEN]->(a:Allergen) WHERE a.name = 'Dairy' WITH count(DISTINCT f2) AS dairycount, totalcount RETURN 1.0 - (toFloat(dairycount) / totalcount)

question: Which pair of food products shares most components (Seasoning, Sweetener, Fat, Allergen), except Main Ingredients? (return names only. the name of food with smaller elementId comes first in return statement)
cypher: MATCH (f1:Food_Product), (f2:Food_Product) WHERE elementId(f1) < elementId(f2) OPTIONAL MATCH (f1)-[:HAS_ALLERGEN|HAS_FAT]->(oc:Allergen|Fat)<-[:HAS_ALLERGEN|HAS_FAT]-(f2) OPTIONAL MATCH (f1)<-[:SEASONS|SWEETENS]-(ic:Seasoning|Sweetener)-[:SEASONS|SWEETENS]->(f2) WITH f1, f2, size(collect(DISTINCT oc)+collect(DISTINCT ic)) AS c RETURN f1.name AS Food1, f2.name AS Food2 ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 1

question: To what probablity that a Sugar-sweetened food product has Dairy as Allergen?
cypher: MATCH (f:Food_Product)<-[:SWEETENS]-(s:Sweetener {name: 'Sugar'}) WITH count(DISTINCT f) AS totalcount MATCH (s:Sweetener {name: 'Sugar'})-[:SWEETENS]->(f2:Food_Product)-[:HAS_ALLERGEN]->(a:Allergen) WHERE a.name = 'Dairy' WITH count(DISTINCT f2) AS dairycount, totalcount RETURN (dairycount / toFloat(totalcount))

question: List 3 pairs of seasonings that appear together most frequently in all the food products. (return names only. Name of seasoning with smaller elementId comes first in return statement.)
cypher: MATCH (s1:Seasoning)-[:SEASONS]->(f:Food_Product)<-[:SEASONS]-(s2:Seasoning) WHERE elementId(s1) < elementId(s2) WITH s1.name AS Seasoning1, s2.name AS Seasoning2, COUNT(DISTINCT f) AS sharedCount RETURN Seasoning1 AS s1, Seasoning2 AS s2 ORDER BY sharedCount DESC LIMIT 3

question: To what probability that a randomly selected food product from this database is the one that has no sweetener, has wheat as Allergen, has spices as Seasoning?
cypher: MATCH (f:Food_Product) OPTIONAL MATCH (f)<-[:SWEETENS]-(sw:Sweetener) OPTIONAL MATCH (f)-[:HAS_ALLERGEN]->(aWheat:Allergen) WHERE toLower(aWheat.name) = 'wheat' OPTIONAL MATCH (s:Seasoning)-[:SEASONS]->(f) WHERE toLower(s.name) = 'spices' WITH COUNT(DISTINCT f) AS total, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN aWheat IS NOT NULL AND sw IS NULL AND s IS NOT NULL THEN f END) AS matchCount RETURN CASE WHEN total = 0 THEN 0.0 ELSE toFloat(matchCount) / total END AS probability

question: Which seasoning is more likely to be used in food products containing Chicken as main ingredient? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (s:Seasoning)-[:SEASONS]->(f:Food_Product)-[:HAS_MAIN_INGREDIENT]->(mi:Main_Ingredient {name: 'Chicken'}) WITH s, count(DISTINCT f) as matchedCount RETURN s.name ORDER BY matchedCount DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which food product is the most distinct? (Distinct means a food product's all components (Seasoning, Sweetener, Fat, Allergen) are least shared with others. return names only)
cypher: MATCH (p:Food_Product) OPTIONAL MATCH (p)-[:HAS_ALLERGEN|HAS_FAT]->(co:Allergen|Fat) OPTIONAL MATCH (ci:Seasoning|Sweetener)-[:SEASONS|SWEETENS]->(p) WITH DISTINCT p, collect(DISTINCT elementId(co)) + collect(DISTINCT elementId(ci)) AS compIds UNWIND compIds AS cid MATCH (c) WHERE elementId(c) = cid WITH p, c, size([(other:Food_Product)-[:HAS_ALLERGEN|HAS_FAT]->(c) | other]) - 1 AS outShare, size([(c)-[:SEASONS|SWEETENS]->(other:Food_Product) | other]) - 1 AS inShare WITH p, c, outShare + inShare AS shareCount WITH p, sum(shareCount) AS totalShared RETURN p.name ORDER BY totalShared ASC LIMIT 1

question: Which Sugar-sweetened food product uses most types of seasoning? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (sw:Sweetener {name: 'Sugar'})-[:SWEETENS]->(f:Food_Product) MATCH (s:Seasoning)-[:SEASONS]->(f) WITH f, COUNT(DISTINCT s) AS numSeasoningTypes RETURN f.name ORDER BY numSeasoningTypes DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which seasoning seasons most food products whose main ingredient is not Spinach? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (s:Seasoning)-[:SEASONS]->(f:Food_Product) WHERE NOT EXISTS { (f)-[:HAS_MAIN_INGREDIENT]->(:Main_Ingredient {name:'Spinach'}) } WITH s.name AS seasoning, COUNT(DISTINCT f) AS uses RETURN seasoning ORDER BY uses DESC LIMIT 1

question: How many items not only appear as Sweetener but also as Main Ingredients?
cypher: MATCH (sw:Sweetener), (mi:Main_Ingredient) WHERE toLower(sw.name) = toLower(mi.name) RETURN count(DISTINCT toLower(sw.name))

question: How many food products' fats' or allergens' names contain milk?
cypher: MATCH (f:Food_Product) WHERE EXISTS { MATCH (f)-[:HAS_FAT]->(fat:Fat) WHERE toLower(fat.name) CONTAINS 'milk' } OR EXISTS { MATCH (f)-[:HAS_ALLERGEN]->(all:Allergen) WHERE toLower(all.name) CONTAINS 'milk' } RETURN count(distinct f)

question: How many food products, not using Caramel sweetener, whose names don't contain its main ingredients' names, are in the database?
cypher: MATCH (f:Food_Product) WHERE NOT EXISTS { (:Sweetener { name: 'Caramel' })-[:SWEETENS]->(f) } MATCH (f)-[:HAS_MAIN_INGREDIENT]->(mi:Main_Ingredient) WITH DISTINCT f, collect(mi.name) AS mainIngs WHERE ALL(ingName IN mainIngs WHERE NOT toLower(f.name) CONTAINS toLower(ingName)) RETURN count(f) AS numProducts

question: How many food products, not using Caramel sweetener, whose names contain its allergen's name?
cypher: MATCH (f:Food_Product) WHERE NOT EXISTS { (:Sweetener {name:'Caramel'})-[:SWEETENS]->(f) } MATCH (f)-[:HAS_ALLERGEN]->(a:Allergen) WHERE toLower(f.name) CONTAINS toLower(a.name) RETURN count(DISTINCT f) AS productCount;

question: I like cheese. List all the food products i might like. (Some food products containing cheese might not have a name containing cheese. return names only.)
cypher: MATCH (node)--(f:Food_Product) WHERE toLower(node.name) CONTAINS 'cheese' OR toLower(f.name) CONTAINS 'cheese' RETURN DISTINCT f.name

question: Which drug interacts most other drugs among all drugs? (notes: output name only)
cypher: MATCH (d:Drug)-[:DDI]-(d2:Drug) WITH d.node_name AS drugname, count(distinct d2) AS drugcount RETURN drugname ORDER BY drugcount DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which protein interacts most drugs that are only interacted by this protein? (Notes: return name only)
cypher: MATCH (p:Protein)<-[:DPI]-(d:Drug) WHERE NOT EXISTS { MATCH (d)-[:DPI]->(p2:Protein) where p2 <> p} WITH p.node_name as name, count(d) as c RETURN name ORDER by c DESC LIMIT 1

question: To what probability that given a disease, a drug used off_label in this disease can interact with indicated drug to the same disease? (Only consider diseases that have INDICATION and OFF_LABEL_USE)
cypher: MATCH (olud1:Drug)-[:OFF_LABEL_USE]->(d:Disease)<-[:INDICATION]-(ind1:Drug) WITH count([olud1, ind1]) as total MATCH (olud2:Drug)-[:OFF_LABEL_USE]->(d:Disease)<-[:INDICATION]-(ind2:Drug) WHERE EXISTS {(olud2)-[:DDI]-(ind2)} WITH COUNT([olud2, ind2]) as matched, total RETURN toFloat(matched)/total

question: To what probability that a drug, which can not only interact with proteins but also diseases, can interact with other drugs?
cypher: MATCH (d:Drug) WHERE EXISTS { (d)-[:DPI]->(:Protein) } AND EXISTS { (d)-[:INDICATION|OFF_LABEL_USE|CONTRAINDICATION]->(:Disease) } OPTIONAL MATCH (d)-[:DDI]-(other:Drug) WITH count(DISTINCT d) as total, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN other is not null then d END) as matched RETURN toFloat(matched)/total

question: Which pair of drugs is most similar in terms of the number of diseases they are both indicated, both contraindicated, both off-label used combined? (Notes: pair of drugs must both have three types of edges to diseases. So, use of optional match might not be appropriate. Only output names of pair of drugs. Name of drug of smaller elementId comes first)
cypher: MATCH (d1:Drug), (d2:Drug) WHERE elementId(d1) < elementId(d2) MATCH (d1)-[:CONTRAINDICATION]->(dc:Disease)<-[:CONTRAINDICATION]-(d2) MATCH (d1)-[:INDICATION]->(di:Disease)<-[:INDICATION]-(d2) MATCH (d1)-[:OFF_LABEL_USE]->(do:Disease)<-[:OFF_LABEL_USE]-(d2) WITH count(DISTINCT dc)+count(DISTINCT di)+count(DISTINCT do) as total, d1, d2 RETURN d1.node_name, d2.node_name ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 1

question: To what probability that a protein which can interact with other proteins can also interact with drugs? (return probability only)
cypher: MATCH (p:Protein) WHERE EXISTS {(p)-[:PPI]-(:Protein)} OPTIONAL MATCH (p)<-[:DPI]-(d:Drug) with count(DISTINCT p) as total, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN d is not null then p END) as matched RETURN toFloat(matched)/total

question: Which disease has most drugs that could be indicated?
cypher: MATCH (d:Drug)-[:INDICATION]->(dis:Disease) WITH dis, COUNT(d) AS drug_count RETURN dis.name AS disease_name ORDER BY drug_count DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which drug is most frequently used off-label to diseases? (output name only)
cypher: MATCH (d:Drug)-[:OFF_LABEL_USE]->(dis:Disease) WITH d, COUNT(distinct dis) AS off_label_count RETURN d.node_name AS drug_name ORDER BY off_label_count DESC LIMIT 1

question: How many diseases that no drug can be indicated?
cypher: MATCH (dis:Disease) WHERE NOT EXISTS { (:Drug)-[:INDICATION]->(dis) } RETURN COUNT(DISTINCT dis)

question: How many diseases whose indicated drugs can interact with each other?
cypher: MATCH (d1:Drug)-[:INDICATION]->(dis:Disease)<-[:INDICATION]-(d2:Drug) WHERE elementId(d1) < elementId(d2) AND EXISTS {(d1)-[:DDI]-(d2)} RETURN COUNT(DISTINCT dis)

question: To what probability that given a specific disease, using two different indicated drugs would result in interaction of these two drugs? (return probability only)
cypher: MATCH (d1:Drug)-[:INDICATION]->(dis:Disease)<-[:INDICATION]-(d2:Drug) WHERE elementId(d1) < elementId(d2) AND EXISTS {(d1)-[:DDI]-(d2)} WITH COUNT([d1, d2]) AS interacting_cases MATCH (d3:Drug)-[:INDICATION]->(dis2:Disease)<-[:INDICATION]-(d4:Drug) WHERE elementId(d3) < elementId(d4) WITH interacting_cases, COUNT([d3, d4]) AS total_pairs RETURN toFloat(interacting_cases) / toFloat(total_pairs)

question: Which country has the highest average imdb_score of movies and shows? (notes: shows' or movies' imdb_score should not be null)
cypher: CALL () { MATCH (c:Country)<-[:PRODUCED_IN]-(m:Movie) WHERE m.imdb_score IS NOT NULL WITH c.country AS country, toFloat(m.imdb_score) AS score RETURN score, country UNION ALL MATCH (c:Country)<-[:PRODUCED_IN]-(s:Show) WHERE s.imdb_score IS NOT NULL WITH c.country AS country, toFloat(s.imdb_score) AS score RETURN score, country } WITH country, avg(score) AS avg_imdb_score RETURN country ORDER BY avg_imdb_score DESC LIMIT 1

question: In a movie or show, if an actor's coactors acted in movies or shows, other than this one, pairwise, we call this actor type A actor. Which type A actor has most these pairs? (actor's coactors exclude this actor)
cypher: MATCH (a:Actor)-[:ACTED_IN]->(work:Movie|Show) MATCH (co1:Actor)-[:ACTED_IN]->(work)<-[:ACTED_IN]-(co2:Actor) WHERE co1 <> a AND elementId(co1) < elementId(co2) AND co2 <> a AND EXISTS { MATCH (co1)-[:ACTED_IN]->(sth)<-[:ACTED_IN]-(co2) WHERE sth <> work} WITH a.name AS name, count(DISTINCT [co1, co2]) AS total RETURN name, total ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which pair of coactors, who collaborated with Robert De Niro (in movies or shows), acted together in movies most frequently? (return names only. Name of actor of smaller elementId comes first in return statement)
cypher: MATCH (rdn:Actor {name: 'Robert De Niro'}) MATCH (rdn)-[:ACTED_IN]->(w)<-[:ACTED_IN]-(co:Actor) WHERE co <> rdn WITH collect(DISTINCT co) AS coactors UNWIND coactors AS b UNWIND coactors AS c WITH b, c WHERE elementId(b) < elementId(c) MATCH (b)-[:ACTED_IN]->(m:Movie)<-[:ACTED_IN]-(c) WITH b, c, count(DISTINCT m) AS movies_together WHERE movies_together > 0 RETURN b.name AS actor1, c.name AS actor2 ORDER BY movies_together DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which genre's movies have the highest average tmdb_score? (return genre only. tmdb_score should not be null)
cypher: MATCH (m:Movie)-[:OF_GENRE]->(g:Genre) WHERE m.tmdb_score IS NOT NULL WITH g.genre AS genre, toFloat(m.tmdb_score) AS score WITH genre, avg(score) AS avg_tmdb RETURN genre ORDER BY avg_tmdb DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which pair of directors directed most movies or shows with age restriction of PG together? (return names only. Name of director with smaller elementId comes first)
cypher: MATCH (d1:Director)-[:DIRECTED]->(w)<-[:DIRECTED]-(d2:Director) MATCH (w)-[:OF_AGE_RESTRICTION]->(ar:Age_Restriction {age_restriction: 'PG'}) WHERE elementId(d1) < elementId(d2) WITH d1.name AS director1, d2.name AS director2, count(DISTINCT w) AS works_together WHERE works_together > 0 RETURN director1, director2 ORDER BY works_together DESC, director1, director2 LIMIT 1

question: Which pair of director and actor collaborated most frequently in movies or shows from US? (return names only, director's name comes first then actor'name. If a director has a good connection with an actor, actor would frequently act in whatever is directed by this director)
cypher: MATCH (d:Director)-[:DIRECTED]->(w)<-[:ACTED_IN]-(a:Actor) MATCH (w)-[:PRODUCED_IN]->(c:Country {country: 'US'}) WITH d.name AS director, a.name AS actor, count(DISTINCT w) AS collaborations RETURN director, actor ORDER BY collaborations DESC, director, actor LIMIT 1

question: Which country most likely produces movies or shows with age restriction of R?
cypher: MATCH (c:Country)<-[:PRODUCED_IN]-(sth:Movie|Show)-[:OF_AGE_RESTRICTION]->(:Age_Restriction {age_restriction: 'R'}) WITH c.country as name, count(DISTINCT sth) as c RETURN name, c ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which show without age restriction has most countries? (return title only)
cypher: MATCH (s:Show) WHERE NOT (s)-[:OF_AGE_RESTRICTION]->(:Age_Restriction) MATCH (s)-[:PRODUCED_IN]->(c:Country) WITH s.title AS title, count(DISTINCT c) AS country_count RETURN title ORDER BY country_count DESC, title LIMIT 1;

question: If a movie with age restriction of R is selected, Which genre of this movie is most likely to be? (return genre only)
cypher: MATCH (m:Movie)-[:OF_AGE_RESTRICTION]->(:Age_Restriction {age_restriction: 'R'}) MATCH (m)-[:OF_GENRE]->(g:Genre) WITH g.genre AS genre, count(m) AS freq RETURN genre ORDER BY freq DESC, genre LIMIT 1

question: Which actor had been to most countries during acting in movies without age restriction?(return name only)
cypher: MATCH (a:Actor)-[:ACTED_IN]->(m:Movie)-[:PRODUCED_IN]->(c:Country) WHERE NOT (m)-[:OF_AGE_RESTRICTION]->(:Age_Restriction) WITH a.name AS actorName, count(DISTINCT c) AS countryCount RETURN actorName ORDER BY countryCount DESC LIMIT 1

question: To what probability that a movie from DE is of genre of comedy? (return probability only)
cypher: MATCH (m:Movie)-[:PRODUCED_IN]->(:Country {country: 'DE'}) OPTIONAL MATCH (m)-[r:OF_GENRE]->(:Genre {genre: 'comedy'}) WITH count(DISTINCT m) as total, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN r is not null then m end) as matched RETURN toFloat(matched)/total

question: An actor might act in movies of different age restrictions. Which actor experienced widest range of age restrictions during acting in movies? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (a:Actor)-[:ACTED_IN]->(m:Movie)-[:OF_AGE_RESTRICTION]->(ar:Age_Restriction) WITH a.name AS actor, count(DISTINCT ar.age_restriction) AS restriction_variety RETURN actor ORDER BY restriction_variety DESC, actor LIMIT 1;

question: To what probability that an actor only acted in movie or show once? (return probability only)
cypher: MATCH (b:Actor) WITH COUNT(DISTINCT b) as total CALL() { MATCH (a:Actor)-[r:ACTED_IN]->(sth:Movie|Show) WITH a, count(r) as edge_count RETURN size(collect(DISTINCT case when edge_count = 1 then a end)) AS matched } RETURN matched/toFloat(total)

question: How many types of machines are there across all datacenters?
cypher: MATCH (dc:DataCenter)-[:CONTAINS]->(:Rack)-[:HOLDS]->(:Machine)-[:TYPE]->(t:Type) RETURN count(DISTINCT t)

question: Which datacenter uses widest range of different OS, whose version is greater than 4.0? (Versions' names are not integers. Don't use toFloat)
cypher: MATCH (dc:DataCenter)-[:CONTAINS]->(:Rack)-[:HOLDS]->(:Machine)-[:RUNS]->(os:OS)-[:INSTANCE]->(v:Version) WHERE v.name > '4.0' WITH dc, COUNT(DISTINCT os.name) AS osCount RETURN dc.name ORDER BY osCount DESC LIMIT 1

question: To what probability that an application depending on neo4j process also depends on java process?
cypher: MATCH (app:Application)-[:DEPENDS_ON]->(p:Process {name: 'neo4j'}) OPTIONAL MATCH (app)-[r:DEPENDS_ON]->(p2:Process {name: 'java'}) WITH count(DISTINCT app) as total, count(DISTINCT case when r is not null then app end) as matched RETURN toFloat(matched)/total

question: To what probability that an application depending on webserver service and php service is using elasticsearch process?
cypher: MATCH (s1:Service {name:'php'})<-[:DEPENDS_ON]-(item:Application)-[:DEPENDS_ON]->(s2:Service {name: 'webserver'}) OPTIONAL MATCH (item)-[r:DEPENDS_ON]->(p:Process {name: 'elasticsearch'}) WITH count(DISTINCT item) as total, count(DISTINCT case when r is not null then item end) as matched RETURN toFloat(matched)/total

question: Which rack's machines run processes that are listening most ports combined? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (r:Rack)-[:HOLDS]->(m:Machine)-[:RUNS]->(p:Process)-[:LISTENS]->(port:Port) WITH r, COUNT(DISTINCT port) AS totalPorts ORDER BY totalPorts DESC LIMIT 1 RETURN r.name AS rackName

question: Which Service or Software or Application or Process has most one-degree dependencies? (A dependency could a software, application, process or service. return the name only)
cypher: MATCH (n:Application|Service|Process|Software)-[:DEPENDS_ON]->(dep:Application|Service|Process|Software) WITH n, COUNT(DISTINCT dep) AS depCount ORDER BY depCount DESC LIMIT 1 RETURN n.name AS name

question: What is the ratio of applications depending on couchbase service and applications depending on neo4j service? (return floating point number only)
cypher: MATCH (a:Application)-[:DEPENDS_ON]->(s:Service) WHERE toLower(s.name) = 'couchbase' WITH count(DISTINCT a) AS couchbaseCount MATCH (a:Application)-[:DEPENDS_ON]->(s:Service) WHERE toLower(s.name) = 'neo4j' WITH count(DISTINCT a) AS neo4jCount, couchbaseCount RETURN couchbaseCount * 1.0 / neo4jCount AS ratio

question: How many different end points are there in this database? (an end point is made up of ip address and port. for example, 127.0.0.1:8080 is an end point)
cypher: MATCH (i:Interface)-[:EXPOSES]->(p:Port) RETURN count(DISTINCT i.ip + ':' + toString(p.port)) AS endpoints

question: To what probability that an application depending on processes which are not listening to any port listens to ports?
cypher: MATCH (app:Application)-[:DEPENDS_ON]->(proc:Process) WHERE NOT EXISTS { (proc)-[:LISTENS]->(:Port) } OPTIONAL MATCH (app)-[r:LISTENS]->(:Port) with count(DISTINCT app) as total, count(DISTINCT case when r is not null then app end) as matched RETURN toFloat(matched)/total

question: An end point is the concatenation of IP and port. For example, 127.0.0.1:8080 is an end point. Which network's ip exposes most end points? (First, network routes to interface, which exposes no port but is connected by incoming edges with other interfaces which expose ports)
cypher: MATCH (n:Network)-[:ROUTES]->(i:Interface)<-[:CONNECTS]-(:Interface)-[:EXPOSES]->(p:Port) with n.ip as ip, count(DISTINCT i.ip + ':' + toString(p.port)) AS endpoints RETURN ip ORDER by endpoints desc LIMIT 1

question: List all the ports that are always being listened by neo4j processes in this database. (return port numbers in a column, which are ordered by port numbers ascendingly.)
cypher: MATCH (p:Process{name: 'neo4j'}) WITH count(DISTINCT p) AS totalProcs MATCH (p:Process {name: 'neo4j'})-[:LISTENS]->(port:Port) WITH port.port AS portNumber, count(DISTINCT p) AS procsListening, totalProcs WHERE procsListening = totalProcs RETURN portNumber ORDER BY portNumber ASC;

question: Which crime is investigated by most officers? (return id only)
cypher: MATCH (c:Crime)-[:INVESTIGATED_BY]->(o:Officer) WITH c.id AS crimeId, count(DISTINCT o) AS officerCount ORDER BY officerCount DESC LIMIT 1 RETURN crimeId;

question: How many persons are involved in crimes which are related to vehicles on average?
cypher: MATCH (v:Vehicle)-[:INVOLVED_IN]->(c:Crime)<-[:PARTY_TO]-(p:Person) WITH c, count(DISTINCT p) AS personsInCrime RETURN avg(personsInCrime)

question: Which officer investigated the crime relating to most phone calls? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (officer:Officer)<-[:INVESTIGATED_BY]-(c:Crime)<-[:PARTY_TO]-(:Person)-[:HAS_PHONE]->(:Phone)<-[:CALLER|CALLED]-(pc:PhoneCall) WITH DISTINCT officer.name as o, count(DISTINCT pc) as phonecalls RETURN o ORDER by phonecalls DESC LIMIT 1

question: What's the area at which most crimes occurred? (return id only)
cypher: MATCH (c:Crime)-[:OCCURRED_AT]->(:Location)-[:LOCATION_IN_AREA]->(a:AREA) WITH a.id AS areaId, count(c) AS totalCrimes ORDER BY totalCrimes DESC LIMIT 1 RETURN areaId

question: Which area has least average number of crimes per location? (return area id only. If multiple areas have the same least crimes, order them by their area ids descendingly.)
cypher: MATCH (a:AREA)<-[:LOCATION_IN_AREA]-(l:Location)<-[:OCCURRED_AT]-(c:Crime) WITH a.id AS areaId, l, count(c) AS crimesAtLocation WITH areaId, avg(crimesAtLocation) AS avgCrimesPerArea ORDER BY avgCrimesPerArea ASC, areaId DESC LIMIT 1 RETURN areaId

question: What is the ratio of crimes which are involving vehicles and crimes which are not involving vehicles?
cypher: MATCH (c:Crime) WITH EXISTS { MATCH (c)<-[:INVOLVED_IN]-(:Vehicle) } AS hasVehicle WITH sum(CASE WHEN hasVehicle THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS withVeh, sum(CASE WHEN hasVehicle THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) AS withoutVeh RETURN CASE WHEN withoutVeh = 0 THEN null ELSE 1.0 * withVeh / withoutVeh END

question: To what probability that a crime which involves phone calls also involves email
cypher: MATCH (c:Crime)<-[:PARTY_TO]-(:Person)-[:HAS_PHONE]->(ph:Phone) MATCH (pc:PhoneCall)-[:CALLER|CALLED]->(ph) OPTIONAL MATCH (c)<-[:PARTY_TO]-(:Person)-[:HAS_EMAIL]->(e:Email) WITH count(DISTINCT c) as total, count(DISTINCT case when e is not null then c end) as matched RETURN toFloat(matched) / total

question: How many officers who investigated crimes are there in each area on average? (duplicate allowed)
cypher: MATCH (a:AREA)<-[:LOCATION_IN_AREA]-(l:Location)<-[:OCCURRED_AT]-(c:Crime)-[:INVESTIGATED_BY]->(o:Officer) WITH a, COUNT(DISTINCT o) AS officers_per_area with avg(officers_per_area) as average RETURN average

question: What is the percentage of police officers who investigated crimes that are involving objects?
cypher: MATCH (o:Officer)<-[:INVESTIGATED_BY]-(c:Crime) OPTIONAL MATCH (c)<-[r:INVOLVED_IN]-(obj:Object) WITH count(DISTINCT o) as total, count(DISTINCT case when r is not null then o end) as matched RETURN toFloat(matched)*100.0/total

question: Which person was involved in most crimes? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (p:Person)-[:PARTY_TO]->(c:Crime) WITH p, COUNT(DISTINCT c) AS crime_count RETURN p.name AS name ORDER BY crime_count DESC LIMIT 1

question: A crime is called isolated crime if all persons involved in this crime were only involved in this crime. How many isolated crimes are there?
cypher: MATCH (c:Crime)<-[:PARTY_TO]-(p:Person) WHERE NOT EXISTS { MATCH (p)-[:PARTY_TO]->(other:Crime) WHERE other <> c} RETURN COUNT(c) AS isolated_crime_count

question: Which crime involves most postcodes? (return crime id only. considering postcodes of persons involved and postcode of location in which crime occured)
cypher: MATCH (pc2:PostCode)<-[:HAS_POSTCODE]-(l2:Location)<-[:CURRENT_ADDRESS]-(person:Person)-[:PARTY_TO]->(c:Crime)-[:OCCURRED_AT]->(l1:Location)-[:HAS_POSTCODE]->(pc1:PostCode) WITH c, count(DISTINCT pc1)+COUNT(DISTINCT pc2) AS num_postcodes RETURN c.id AS crime_id ORDER BY num_postcodes DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which person knows or is known by most other persons that are involved in crimes? (return name only)
cypher: MATCH (p:Person)-[:KNOWS]-(other:Person)-[:PARTY_TO]->(:Crime) WITH DISTINCT p, count(DISTINCT other) AS num_contacts RETURN p.name AS name ORDER BY num_contacts DESC LIMIT 1
